This is a list of Pokémon that are available in Pokémon Champions. 208 Pokémon species are available in the game, as well as 76 Mega Evolutions.

Functionally distinct, permanent forms (such as regional forms) are listed alongside the base forms of their respective species, while other forms (such as Mega Evolutions) are listed in separate sections below.

Until September 2, 2026, the current roster is Regular Roster M-B. Pokémon not in the current roster cannot be obtained without the use of events or transferring them from another game.

Contents

  • 1 List of Pokémon in Champions
    • 1.1 Forms
      • 1.1.1 Mega Evolutions
      • 1.1.2 Other forms
  • 2 Untransferable Pokémon
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Related articles

List of Pokémon in Champions[edit]

Eternal Flower Floette is allowed under the current regulation, but it must be transferred from Legends: Z-A through HOME.

Ndex MS Pokémon Type Normally available? Version added

Forms[edit]

Mega Evolutions[edit]

Some Mega Stones are only obtainable from the tutorial, rather than the shop. The Floettite stone is allowed under the current regulation, but it requires transferring the base Pokémon from HOME.

Ndex MS Pokémon Type Normally available? Version added

Mega Venusaur 		Grass Poison Yes 1.0.2

  • Pikachu in a cap and Greninja with Battle Bond cannot be sent to Pokémon Champions.

Untransferable Pokémon[edit]

These Pokémon are present in the game data, but they cannot currently be transferred in from HOME.

Ndex MS Pokémon Type Normally available? Version added
#0923 Pawmot
 Electric Fighting No 1.0.2

Trivia[edit]

  • Some Pokémon were not present in the game at release, despite being shown in promotional material or artwork related to Pokémon Champions:
    • Metagross, Ursaluna, Dondozo, Tatsugiri, and Mega Raichu X were shown in promotional material.
    • Mega Raichu Y and Mega Zygarde were shown on the official art for the 2026 Japan Championships, which was played on Pokémon Champions.
    • Salamence is featured on the first splash screen.
      • Excluding Legendary and Mythical Pokémon, Mega Salamence is the only Mega Evolution introduced in Generation VI that is not present in Pokémon Champions.
    • Pawmot is featured on the "Battle" icon on the menu (unlike the others, however, it is present in the game's code, but it is not playable).
  • No Legendary or Mythical Pokémon are present in the game.
    • Mega Zygarde is the only Mega Evolution introduced in the base game of Pokémon Legends: Z-A to not be present in Pokémon Champions.
  • Some Pokémon in the game have unique distinctions:
    • Pikachu is the only Pokémon present in the game that is capable of Evolution.
    • Qwilfish is the only Pokémon present in the game which has a regional form that is not present in the game.
    • Castform is the only Pokémon present in the game that does not appear in any Generation IX core series games.

Related articles[edit]

  • Pokémon Champions
Pokémon availability
Core series List of Pokémon by availability
Available in every game • Version-exclusive • Unobtainable in later games
Mutually exclusive • Permanently missable • Unobtainable Shiny
PE (See more) • SwSh • BDSP • LA (See more) • SV • ZA
In-game event Pokémon Gen I • Gen II • Gen III (RS • FRLG • E • Colosseum/XD) • Gen IV (DP • Pt • HGSS) • Gen V (BW • B2W2)
Gen VI (XY • ORAS) • Gen VII (SM • USUM • PE) • Gen VIII (SwSh • BDSP • LA) • Gen IX (SV • ZA)

Side series Champions
